How this model works
ComponentWhat it measuresHow this model uses it
EMA100 & slope Medium‑term trend direction. Compares price to the 100‑day EMA and looks at the EMA's recent slope. Price above a rising EMA100 = Uptrend; below a flat/falling EMA100 = Downtrend; mixed cases are Deteriorating or Recovering. This regime sets how aggressive you're allowed to be (only Uptrend can reach accumulate).
MACD (line, signal, histogram) Short‑ vs medium‑term momentum and whether that momentum is strengthening or weakening. The MACD line is "fast 12‑day EMA minus slow 26‑day EMA," so it shows how hot/cold recent price is versus the baseline. The signal line (9‑day EMA of MACD) is a smoothed average of the MACD line. The histogram is the gap between them. MACD is supportive when the MACD line is above the signal line and the histogram is rising (bullish momentum building, especially if MACD > 0), and contradicting when the line is below the signal line and the histogram is falling (bearish momentum building). Simple line crossovers, without the histogram confirming, are not treated as reliable reversal signals.
RSI(20) Balance of recent buying vs selling (0–100 scale). Looks at average gains vs losses over ~1 month. The same number means different things in different regimes: in an Uptrend, mid‑to‑high RSI (roughly 50–65) is "healthy" and >70 is "extended/chasing"; in a Downtrend, low RSI is "stretched but can still fall" and >50 is only early repair, not a full green light. This helps separate a buyable pullback in a strong trend from a value‑trap bounce in a weak one.
Bollinger %B (position in volatility band) Where today's price sits inside a volatility‑adjusted "normal range." Bollinger Bands create a moving "channel" around price based on a moving average and recent volatility. %B translates that into a 0–1+ score: 0 at the lower band, 1 at the upper band, <0 below the band, >1 above it. In an Uptrend, being near the lower band (low %B) can mark a buyable pullback because the underlying trend is still constructive. In a Downtrend, the exact same low %B is treated as "price is still sliding toward the weak end of its range," not a bargain. Very high %B (near or above the upper band) marks "extended" conditions and caps the action at wait so you don't chase.
Volume vs 20‑day avg Conviction behind today's move. Compares today's volume to a 20‑day average to see if there is unusually strong participation. High volume on an up‑day = constructive confirmation; high volume on a down‑day = distribution. Light volume means the price move gets less weight. It never acts alone; it just upgrades or downgrades the confidence you place in the price/momentum signals.
Regime & veto rules Overall consistency of signals. The regime (from EMA100), plus how many indicators are supportive vs conflicting, plus a few hard rules (e.g., no accumulate outside an Uptrend; ≥3 conflicting signals forces wait) roll up into one of four tactical actions: accumulate, starter buy, wait, or avoid adding, each with a confidence level.
